Class SceneHandler

java.lang.Object
application.SceneHandler
public class SceneHandler extends Object
Utility class that owns a single JavaFX Scene and handles root swapping.

Keeps scene initialization centralized so different screens can transition without creating new stage/scene instances.

Usage pattern:

  1. Call init(Stage, Parent) once during app startup.
  2. Switch views by calling switchRoot(Parent) with new roots.
  3. Access the shared scene via getScene() if needed.

  • Constructor Details

    • SceneHandler

      private SceneHandler()
      Prevents instantiation of this utility class.

  • Method Details

    • init

      public static void init(javafx.stage.Stage primaryStage, javafx.scene.Parent firstRoot)
      Initializes the scene graph once and binds it to the primary stage.

      This method should be called exactly once at application startup before any call to switchRoot(Parent).

      Parameters:
      primaryStage - JavaFX primary stage
      firstRoot - initial root node shown in the scene

    • switchRoot

      public static void switchRoot(javafx.scene.Parent newRoot)
      Replaces current scene root with a new root and forces immediate layout pass.

      Calling applyCss() and layout() ensures the new root has styles and layout computed before the next render pass.

      Parameters:
      newRoot - new root node to display

    • getScene

      public static javafx.scene.Scene getScene()
      Returns the shared scene instance.
      Returns:
      active scene
